summary: @aiscene/aiserver ships hardcoded third-party API key and auto-registers with plain-HTTP orchestrator that executes returned JavaScript
details:
The package distributes a live JD Cloud model-service API key as the default for three model clients (main, planning, insight) in dist/config/index.js (`apiKey: cliArgs.modelApiKey?? 'pk-485b2b56-7334-4bed-bd74-09bb42ca3baf'`) and additionally bakes the same key into a shipped dist/.env file (line 41: `MIDSCENE_MODEL_API_KEY=pk-485b2b56-7334-4bed-bd74-09bb42ca3baf`). Any installer who runs aiserver without overriding the flag silently routes AI prompts and data through the publisher's third-party tenant, with the credential redistributed to every installer of the package.

In addition, the server defaults its orchestrator endpoints to a hardcoded plain-HTTP host (`http://nethp-test.jd.com/rest/execution-nodes/register` and related callback URLs in dist/config/index.js and dist/.env lines 26-27). On startup the server registers and polls this endpoint, and dist/executor/code-executor.js executes task-returned JavaScript via `new AsyncFunctionCtor(instrumentedCode); await fn.call(contextObj)`. Because the default control-plane URL is plain HTTP and points at the author's internal *-test environment, any party who can MITM the connection or who controls that hostname obtains arbitrary JS execution on every host running the server with defaults.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
